ideas.title: Evolutionary Game Theory for Adaptive Adversarial Navigation
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution from evolutionary biology, specifically evolutionary game theory, to the problem of adversarial planning. In biology, strategies within a population evolve toward stable equilibria (ESS) based on reproductive fitness from repeated interactions. The adapted research idea applies this framework to robotic or autonomous agent navigation in adversarial settings. Here, the 'population' is the set of interacting agents, and their navigation strategies (like contesting a choke point or yielding) evolve based on success payoffs (e.g., time to goal, collision avoidance). This allows for the development of adaptive planning algorithms where agents dynamically adjust their behavior based on the evolving strategy mix of adversaries, leading to more robust and emergent navigation solutions in complex, competitive environments.

ideas.title: Information Geometric Filter Design via Thermodynamic Control Analogy
ideas.description: This idea expands the original seed by drawing an analogy from non-equilibrium thermodynamics. In that field, geometric methods on statistical manifolds are used to find optimal control protocols that minimize dissipation (like entropy production) when driving a system between states. We adapt this solution to filter design. Here, the family of possible filters (parameterized by coefficients, bandwidth, etc.) forms a statistical manifold. Designing an adaptive filter—one that must change its characteristics over time—is analogous to driving a thermodynamic system. Using information geometry (Fisher information metric, divergences), we can define a cost functional for the filter's trajectory that penalizes abrupt, inefficient changes. The research involves formulating this optimal control problem on the filter manifold, deriving the geometric equations for the minimal-dissipation path, and applying it to design adaptive filters with superior transient performance and stability for signal processing and communications.

ideas.title: Resilient Multi-Robot Belief Coordination under Communication Degradation and Adversarial Faults
ideas.description: This research develops a robust coordination framework that guarantees safe, convergent team behavior despite unreliable communication and the presence of faulty or adversarial agents. It integrates Byzantine-resilient distributed consensus algorithms with local probabilistic filters and planners, decoupling resilient agreement from specific estimation models. The system provides formal guarantees on belief convergence and safety under bounded network faults, enabling provably reliable coordination in adversarial or degraded real-world environments like search-and-rescue or contested operations.

ideas.title: Passive, Large-Scale Physiological Sensing from Uncontrolled Motion
ideas.description: This research fuses passive sensing with large-scale data diversity to create models that estimate physiological states (e.g., heart rate, stress) from uncontrolled, incidental motion in video or wearable data. By training on massive, demographically diverse datasets of passive recordings—capturing people in natural settings with varied motion, lighting, skin tones, and activities—the models learn to disentangle physiological signals from motion artifacts and confounding variables. This synergy yields systems that generalize across populations without requiring user calibration or controlled actuation, enabling scalable, unobtrusive health sensing in everyday environments like homes, offices, and public spaces.

ideas.title: Prospective validation of a multi-modal, patient-stratified AI diagnostic assistant for clinical screening
ideas.description: This research develops and prospectively validates a multi-modal AI diagnostic system that fuses dermoscopic/clinical images with rich patient metadata (age, lesion history, personal/family cancer history, skin type) in a real-world clinical screening workflow. The core innovation is the prospective trial design, which is specifically powered to test the hypothesis that the multi-modal fusion significantly outperforms both image-only AI and practicing dermatologists, particularly in challenging but clinically vital subgroups such as patients with numerous nevi, a personal history of melanoma, or lesions on anatomically difficult sites. The outcome is a validated, context-aware clinical assistant that provides stratified performance evidence, demonstrating not just if it works, but for whom and in what scenarios it offers the greatest benefit to augment clinical decision-making.

ideas.title: Entropic Path Planning via Ecological Succession Principles
ideas.description: This idea adapts the ecological principle of managing disturbances to maximize successional uncertainty to the problem of deliberate state uncertainty maximization. In ecology, practitioners can design interventions (e.g., patchy burns, introducing pioneer species) to prevent an ecosystem from following a single, predictable recovery path, thereby maximizing its potential future biodiversity and structural uncertainty. Adapted to a planning or estimation context, this involves designing paths or control inputs that act as 'informational disturbances.' These actions are chosen not to gather information but to disperse it—preventing the agent's state estimate from converging, actively countering localization, and maximizing the entropy over possible states. The goal is to systematically explore the dual of localization by scaling up the generation of ambiguity, much like an ecosystem manager scales up biodiversity potential through deliberate, uncertainty-maximizing interventions.

ideas.title: Adversarial Game Theory for Robust AI Validation in Prospective Clinical Trials
ideas.description: This research proposes a novel validation paradigm for clinical AI, such as dermatology screening tools, by integrating game-theoretic adversarial planning into the design of prospective trials. Instead of a simple head-to-head comparison against clinicians, the trial environment is modeled as a game where simulated or red-team adversaries strategically attempt to fool the AI with challenging cases, data perturbations, or evolving disease patterns. This approach rigorously tests the model's robustness and failure modes under intelligent pressure, providing a more comprehensive and security-informed assessment of real-world readiness than standard clinical validation alone.

ideas.title: Adaptive Sensorimotor Sampling for Efficient Uncertainty Reduction
ideas.description: Drawing an analogy from sensory ecology and animal foraging strategies, this research adapts the principle of energy-efficient, uncertainty-directed sampling to robotic active perception. In nature, animals like echolocating bats dynamically adjust their sensing rate and flight path based on acoustic feedback to locate prey, a process modeled by infotaxis and related theories. The adapted idea is to design control policies where an agent's actions—both macroscopic movement and fine-grained sensor manipulation—are chosen to maximize the reduction of state uncertainty per unit of control effort or time. This involves creating an objective function that quantifies information gain from potential observation sequences, guiding the agent to adaptively sample its environment. The resulting policy would enable an autonomous system to intelligently gather data, minimizing overall uncertainty in a resource-efficient manner, much like a foraging animal optimally explores its habitat.

ideas.title: Computational Information Geometric Optimization for Adaptive Filter Design
ideas.description: This research develops a concrete design methodology where a filter's action is explicitly modeled as a transformation on a statistical manifold of signal distributions. The core is an optimization loop that uses computationally tractable information geometric tools, such as natural gradient descent or mirror descent, to adapt filter parameters. The objective is to minimize a chosen statistical divergence (e.g., KL, Wasserstein) between a desired target distribution and the filter's output distribution. This results in an adaptive, principled design algorithm that directly translates geometric insight into superior filter performance for specific tasks like signal separation or noise suppression, validated against conventional optimal filters.

ideas.title: Bias-Resistant Differential Diagnosis via Cognitive Forcing and Delayed AI Reconciliation
ideas.description: This system is designed to prevent clinician over-reliance on AI by structuring the diagnostic workflow to prioritize independent clinical reasoning. It first presents the patient's data and key findings, then requires the clinician to manually construct a differential diagnosis before any AI suggestions are shown. The AI then outputs its differential not as a ranked list, but as a set of diagnostic possibilities, each explicitly linked to supporting and contradicting clinical evidence from the case. Finally, the system facilitates a structured reconciliation, comparing the clinician's and AI's differentials, highlighting areas of agreement and discrepancy with evidence, and prompting explicit justification for the final assessment.

ideas.description: This study employs a pragmatic, multi-center clinical trial design to evaluate an AI model for skin lesion assessment across diverse real-world clinical environments, including primary care and dermatology clinics. The core intervention integrates the model into existing clinical workflows to measure its impact on diagnostic accuracy, referral efficiency, and clinician decision-making compared to standard practice. An embedded mixed-methods framework concurrently collects quantitative performance data and qualitative insights from clinicians regarding usability, trust, and workflow adaptation. This holistic approach is designed to validate not just algorithmic performance, but the model's practical utility, failure modes, and overall effect on the clinical pathway.

ideas.title: Passive, Motion-Robust Dermatology from Uncontrolled Smartphone Videos
ideas.description: This research inverts the standard requirement for high-quality, static dermoscopic images by developing models that leverage the uncontrolled motion in casual, consumer-grade smartphone videos for robust skin assessment. Instead of relying on a single poor-quality frame, the method applies principles of passive state estimation to treat the user's natural hand motion as an unactuated sensor sweep, extracting multi-view geometry and photometric consistency to reconstruct a stabilized, 3D texture map of the skin lesion. This synthesized model, derived from inherently noisy data, provides a more information-rich and artifact-reduced representation than any single input frame, enabling accurate analysis without requiring user expertise or specialized hardware. The core innovation is a joint optimization that simultaneously estimates ego-motion, refines scene geometry, and performs the diagnostic classification, all from passive, low-quality video streams.

ideas.title: Byzantine-Robust, Trust-Aware Multi-Agent Collaborative SLAM
ideas.description: This system scales SLAM to thousands of vehicles while guaranteeing robustness against faulty and malicious agents. It employs a decentralized architecture where local maps are fused only after verification through a consensus mechanism that requires redundant independent observations for landmark acceptance. Each agent maintains a trust score for its peers based on historical consistency, dynamically down-weighting or excluding unreliable data sources. The result is a resilient, city-scale dynamic map that is provably secure against data poisoning and sybil attacks.

ideas.title: Passive State Estimation via Environmental Signal Deconvolution
ideas.description: This research inverts the assumption of controlled motion for state estimation by adapting a solution from astrophysics. In radio astronomy, large fixed arrays or single dishes use Earth's rotation (aperture synthesis) and advanced deconvolution algorithms to create high-resolution images without actively steering the telescope. The adapted idea applies this principle to passive sensing platforms (e.g., stationary cameras, floating buoys, or mounted sensors). By modeling the platform's natural, uncontrolled micro-motions (from wind, currents, or structural vibrations) as a known or estimable baseline trajectory, we can treat these motions as a synthetic aperture. The core research challenge is to develop novel estimation algorithms that deconvolve the target state (e.g., object position, shape, or signal source location) from the passively received sensor data, effectively using environmental perturbation as a replacement for designed actuation.

ideas.title: Swarm Localization via Quorum Sensing and Collective Gradient Estimation
ideas.description: This research adapts the principle of bacterial quorum sensing and collective gradient estimation to solve micro-robot swarm localization under severe sensor constraints. In microbial ecology, simple bacteria with no capability for global positioning use the local concentration of diffusible signaling molecules to infer population density and environmental gradient direction, coordinating via biased random walks. The adapted solution replaces chemical signals with low-power, robot-emitted identifier pulses. Each micro-robot is limited to sensing a single scalar: the aggregate signal intensity in its immediate neighborhood. By correlating changes in this local 'quorum' measurement with its own primitive movement actions, each robot can contribute to a swarm-wide process of gradient ascent/descent. This enables the emergent self-organization of the swarm into formations or migration toward targets without any individual robot performing classical localization, triangulation, or requiring complex sensors, scaling the localization problem down to ultra-constrained hardware.

ideas.title: Active Differential Diagnosis via Strategic Uncertainty Exploration
ideas.description: This research designs clinical decision support systems that treat the differential diagnosis not as a static list, but as a dynamic belief state to be actively interrogated. Instead of seeking to localize to a single diagnosis, the system deliberately plans tests, questions, or observations to maximize the uncertainty (e.g., entropy) across the differential, forcing the set of possibilities to diverge and reveal their most distinguishing features. This transforms diagnostic workup from a confirmatory process into an efficient exploration of the diagnostic space, explicitly highlighting the critical decision boundaries for clinician review and reducing the risk of premature closure.

ideas.title: Passive SLAM for Sensor Networks via Seismic Tomography Principles
ideas.description: This research expands the seed idea 'Passive SLAM for sensor networks' by drawing an analogy from geophysics. The analogous problem is seismic tomography, where a static network of seismometers passively records waves to simultaneously image Earth's subsurface structure and locate seismic events. The analogous solution involves solving a large-scale inverse problem using arrival times and waveforms. Adapted to the sensor network context, the research idea is to develop a framework where a static, passive sensor network detects signals from moving targets. By applying tomographic inversion principles, the system would jointly estimate a model of the signal propagation environment (the 'map,' accounting for obstacles, attenuation, and multipath) and the trajectories of the moving targets. This enables scalable, dual-purpose localization and mapping without requiring the sensor network to emit any signals, enhancing stealth and energy efficiency.

ideas.description: This research direction develops a mobile-optimized application that uses a device's camera to capture short video sequences or rapid sequential images of a skin lesion. It processes these frames in real-time to extract and quantify dynamic features, such as blood flow patterns (via subtle color changes), border expansion, or texture evolution. The system provides immediate feedback on the lesion's temporal behavior, aiding in distinguishing benign from malignant growth. This fusion creates a novel diagnostic tool that leverages the ubiquity of mobile devices to perform dynamic, time-based analysis previously confined to specialized clinical imaging systems.

ideas.title: Relative State Inference via Structured Beacon Exchange for Micro-Swarm Localization
ideas.description: This research addresses the fundamental need for relative state estimation in sensor-limited micro-robots by co-designing communication and sensing. Each robot is equipped with a minimalist emitter and detector capable of sending and receiving uniquely patterned optical or RF beacons according to a randomized schedule. By analyzing the interference patterns, signal strength decay, or timing of received neighbor beacons, each agent infers coarse relative distance and direction. This inferred relative state graph then feeds into ultra-constrained, robust localization algorithms, enabling swarm coordination without traditional sensors or external infrastructure.

ideas.title: Ambiguity-Engineered Passive Sensing
ideas.description: This research direction inverts the goal of traditional passive sensing by designing the observed signals or environment to deliberately maximize the state ambiguity of a target entity, such as a vehicle or signal source, from the perspective of a passive observer. It investigates how to structure external stimuli, environmental features, or emission patterns—without active control by the observer—to induce maximal localization uncertainty. This reveals the theoretical upper bounds of uncertainty that can be imposed on a passive estimator, providing a dual perspective on optimal sensing. The synergy enables a new analysis of privacy, security, and fundamental performance limits in passive sensing scenarios where the observer lacks actuation authority.

ideas.title: Prospective, Blinded, Multi-Center Trial of AI Dermatology Model vs. Clinicians Using a Standardized Diagnostic Protocol
ideas.description: This expanded idea adapts the validation framework from cardiology diagnostic trials to test a dermatology AI model. In cardiology, new imaging modalities are validated in prospective, multi-center, blinded studies using a core lab and standardized protocols to ensure unbiased comparison against a gold standard. Adapted here, the AI model would be integrated into a prospective clinical screening trial across multiple dermatology clinics. A central coordinating center would establish a rigorous imaging and data collection protocol (lighting, camera specifications, lesion documentation). Patient cases would be evaluated in real-time by both the on-site dermatologist and the AI system. The definitive outcome measure would be a consensus diagnosis from a blinded, independent expert panel of dermatologists reviewing all available data (including dermoscopy and histopathology if available). This design allows for a direct, fair comparison of the AI's diagnostic accuracy and clinical workflow integration against practicing dermatologists, moving beyond retrospective validation to demonstrate real-world effectiveness and safety.

ideas.title: Adapting Geophysical Time-Series Analysis for Lesion Evolution Tracking
ideas.description: This idea challenges the static image analysis paradigm in dermatology by drawing an analogy from geophysics. In seismology, the solution to understanding complex earth processes involves analyzing continuous time-series data to differentiate event types and model dynamics. Adapting this, the research would apply similar time-series and signal processing techniques (e.g., change-point detection, frequency analysis) to video or sequential image data of skin lesions. The goal is to move beyond static morphological assessment to quantify dynamic changes—like growth patterns, texture evolution, and vascular activity over time—thereby creating a more powerful framework for monitoring lesion behavior and improving early detection of malignant transformation.

papers.title: A Probabilistic Approach to Concurrent Mapping and Localization for Mobile Robots
